mn148:1.2: at one time the *Bhagavā* was dwelling at Sāvatthī, in Jeta's Grove, Anāthapiṇḍika's park.
mn148:10.1: If someone were to say, ‘The eye is the self,’ that is not tenable.
mn148:10.11: if someone were to say, ‘Forms [*rūpā*] are the self.’
mn148:10.12: Thus the eye is not-self [*anattā*], forms [*rūpā*] are not-self [*anattā*].
mn148:10.13: If someone were to say, ‘Eye-*viññāṇa* is the self,’ that is not tenable.
mn148:10.14: The arising and passing away of eye-*viññāṇa* are evident.
mn148:10.15: But for that whose arising and passing away are evident, it would follow for him: ‘My self arises and passes away.’
mn148:10.17: if someone were to say, ‘Eye-*viññāṇa* is the self.’
mn148:10.18: Thus the eye is not-self [*anattā*], forms [*rūpā*] are not-self [*anattā*], eye-*viññāṇa* is not-self [*anattā*].
mn148:10.19: If someone were to say, ‘Eye-contact is the self,’ that is not tenable.
mn148:10.2: The arising and passing away of the eye are evident.
mn148:10.20: The arising and passing away of eye-contact are evident.
mn148:10.21: But for that whose arising and passing away are evident, it would follow for him: ‘My self arises and passes away.’
mn148:10.23: if someone were to say, ‘Eye-contact is the self.’
mn148:10.24: Thus the eye is not-self [*anattā*], forms [*rūpā*] are not-self [*anattā*], eye-*viññāṇa* is not-self [*anattā*], eye-contact is not-self [*anattā*].
mn148:10.25: If someone were to say, ‘*Vedanā* is the self,’ that is not tenable.
mn148:10.26: The arising and passing away of *vedanā* are evident.
mn148:10.27: But for that whose arising and passing away are evident, it would follow for him: ‘My self arises and passes away.’
mn148:10.29: if someone were to say, ‘*Vedanā* is the self.’
mn148:10.3: But for that whose arising and passing away are evident, it would follow for him: ‘My self arises and passes away.’
mn148:10.30: Thus the eye is not-self [*anattā*], forms [*rūpā*] are not-self [*anattā*], eye-*viññāṇa* is not-self [*anattā*], eye-contact is not-self [*anattā*], *vedanā* is not-self [*anattā*].
mn148:10.31: If someone were to say, ‘*Taṇhā* is the self,’ that is not tenable.
mn148:10.32: The arising and passing away of *taṇhā* are evident.
mn148:10.33: But for that whose arising and passing away are evident, it would follow for him: ‘My self arises and passes away.’
mn148:10.35: if someone were to say, ‘*Taṇhā* is the self.’
mn148:10.36: Thus the eye is not-self [*anattā*], forms [*rūpā*] are not-self [*anattā*], eye-*viññāṇa* is not-self [*anattā*], eye-contact is not-self [*anattā*], *vedanā* is not-self [*anattā*], *taṇhā* is not-self [*anattā*].
mn148:10.5: if someone were to say, ‘The eye is the self.’
mn148:10.7: If someone were to say, ‘Forms [*rūpā*] are the self,’ that is not tenable.
mn148:10.8: The arising and passing away of forms [*rūpānaṁ*] are evident.
mn148:10.9: But for that whose arising and passing away are evident, it would follow for him: ‘My self arises and passes away.’
mn148:11.1: If someone were to say, ‘The ear is the self’ …
mn148:11.2: If someone were to say, ‘The nose is the self’ …
mn148:11.3: If someone were to say, ‘The tongue is the self’ …
mn148:11.4: If someone were to say, ‘The body is the self’ …
mn148:11.5: If someone were to say, ‘The mind is the self,’ that is not tenable.
mn148:11.6: The arising and passing away of the mind are evident.
mn148:11.7: But for that whose arising and passing away are evident, it would follow for him: ‘My self arises and passes away.’
mn148:11.9: if someone were to say, ‘The mind is the self.’
mn148:12.1: If someone were to say, ‘*Dhammas* are the self,’ that is not tenable.
mn148:12.2: The arising and passing away of *dhammas* are evident.
mn148:12.3: But for that whose arising and passing away are evident, it would follow for him: ‘My self arises and passes away.’
mn148:12.5: if someone were to say, ‘*Dhammas* are the self.’
mn148:12.6: Thus the mind is not-self [*anattā*], *dhammas* are not-self [*anattā*].
mn148:13.1: If someone were to say, ‘Mind-*viññāṇa* is the self,’ that is not tenable.
mn148:13.2: The arising and passing away of mind-*viññāṇa* are evident.
mn148:13.3: But for that whose arising and passing away are evident, it would follow for him: ‘My self arises and passes away.’
mn148:13.5: if someone were to say, ‘Mind-*viññāṇa* is the self.’
mn148:13.6: Thus the mind is not-self [*anattā*], *dhammas* are not-self [*anattā*], mind-*viññāṇa* is not-self [*anattā*].
mn148:14.1: If someone were to say, ‘Mind-contact is the self,’ that is not tenable.
mn148:14.2: The arising and passing away of mind-contact are evident.
mn148:14.3: But for that whose arising and passing away are evident, it would follow for him: ‘My self arises and passes away.’
mn148:14.5: if someone were to say, ‘Mind-contact is the self.’
mn148:14.6: Thus the mind is not-self [*anattā*], *dhammas* are not-self [*anattā*], mind-*viññāṇa* is not-self [*anattā*], mind-contact is not-self [*anattā*].
mn148:15.1: If someone were to say, ‘*Vedanā* is the self,’ that is not tenable.
mn148:15.11: if someone were to say, ‘*Taṇhā* is the self.’
mn148:15.12: Thus the mind is not-self [*anattā*], *dhammas* are not-self [*anattā*], mind-*viññāṇa* is not-self [*anattā*], mind-contact is not-self [*anattā*], *vedanā* is not-self [*anattā*], *taṇhā* is not-self [*anattā*].
mn148:15.2: The arising and passing away of *vedanā* are evident.
mn148:15.3: But for that whose arising and passing away are evident, it would follow for him: ‘My self arises and passes away.’
mn148:15.5: if someone were to say, ‘*Vedanā* is the self.’
mn148:15.6: Thus the mind is not-self [*anattā*], *dhammas* are not-self [*anattā*], mind-*viññāṇa* is not-self [*anattā*], mind-contact is not-self [*anattā*], *vedanā* is not-self [*anattā*].
mn148:15.7: If someone were to say, ‘*Taṇhā* is the self,’ that is not tenable.
mn148:15.8: The arising and passing away of *taṇhā* are evident.
mn148:15.9: But for that whose arising and passing away are evident, it would follow for him: ‘My self arises and passes away.’
mn148:16.1: And this, *bhikkhus*, is the practice leading to the arising of identity—
mn148:16.2: one regards the eye thus: ‘This is mine, this I am, this is my self’;
mn148:17-21.1: one regards forms [*rūpe*] thus: ‘This is mine, this I am, this is my self’;
mn148:17-21.10: one regards the mind thus: ‘This is mine, this I am, this is my self’; one regards *dhammas* thus: ‘This is mine, this I am, this is my self’; one regards mind-*viññāṇa* thus: ‘This is mine, this I am, this is my self’; one regards mind-contact thus: ‘This is mine, this I am, this is my self’; one regards *vedanā* thus: ‘This is mine, this I am, this is my self’; one regards *taṇhā* thus: ‘This is mine, this I am, this is my self.’
mn148:17-21.2: one regards eye-*viññāṇa* thus: ‘This is mine, this I am, this is my self’;
mn148:17-21.3: one regards eye-contact thus: ‘This is mine, this I am, this is my self’;
mn148:17-21.4: one regards *vedanā* thus: ‘This is mine, this I am, this is my self’;
mn148:17-21.5: one regards *taṇhā* thus: ‘This is mine, this I am, this is my self’;
mn148:17-21.6: one regards the ear thus: ‘This is mine, this I am, this is my self’ …
mn148:17-21.7: one regards the nose thus: ‘This is mine, this I am, this is my self’ …
mn148:17-21.8: one regards the tongue thus: ‘This is mine, this I am, this is my self’ …
mn148:17-21.9: one regards the body thus: ‘This is mine, this I am, this is my self’ …
mn148:2.1: “*Bhikkhus*, I will teach you a *dhamma* that is good in the beginning, good in the middle, and good in the end, with meaning and phrasing; I will reveal the holy life, entirely complete and pure, namely—
mn148:2.3: Listen to that, attend carefully, and I will speak.”
mn148:22.1: And this, *bhikkhus*, is the practice leading to the cessation of identity—
mn148:22.2: one regards the eye thus: ‘This is not mine, this I am not, this is not my self.’

mn148:23-27.3: One regards eye-contact thus: ‘This is not mine, this I am not, this is not my self.’
mn148:23-27.4: One regards *vedanā* thus: ‘This is not mine, this I am not, this is not my self.’

mn148:28.1: Dependent on the eye, *bhikkhus*, and forms [*rūpe*], eye-*viññāṇa* arises; the meeting of the three is contact; with contact as condition there arises what is felt: *sukha* or *dukkha* or neither-*dukkha*-nor-*sukha*.
mn148:28.2: Touched by *sukha vedanā*, he delights in it, welcomes it, and remains holding to it.
mn148:28.3: His underlying tendency to passion underlies it.
mn148:28.4: Touched by *dukkha vedanā*, he sorrows, becomes weary, laments, beats his breast, cries, and falls into confusion.
mn148:28.5: His underlying tendency to resistance underlies it.
mn148:28.6: Touched by neither-*dukkha*-nor-*sukha vedanā*, he does not understand as it truly is the arising, passing away, gratification, danger, and escape in regard to that *vedanā*.
mn148:28.7: His underlying tendency to blindness [*avijjā*] underlies it.
mn148:28.8: That, *bhikkhus*, without abandoning the underlying tendency to passion toward *sukha vedanā*, without driving out the underlying tendency to resistance toward *dukkha vedanā*, without uprooting the underlying tendency to blindness [*avijjā*] toward neither-*dukkha*-nor-*sukha vedanā*, without abandoning blindness [*avijjā*], without giving rise to knowledge, he will make an end of *dukkha* in this very life—there is no such possibility.
mn148:29-33.1: Dependent on the ear, *bhikkhus*, and sounds, ear-*viññāṇa* arises …
mn148:29-33.10: Touched by neither-*dukkha*-nor-*sukha vedanā*, he does not understand as it truly is the arising, passing away, gratification, danger, and escape in regard to that *vedanā*.
mn148:29-33.11: His underlying tendency to blindness [*avijjā*] underlies it.
mn148:29-33.12: That, *bhikkhus*, without abandoning the underlying tendency to passion toward *sukha vedanā*, without driving out the underlying tendency to resistance toward *dukkha vedanā*, without uprooting the underlying tendency to blindness [*avijjā*] toward neither-*dukkha*-nor-*sukha vedanā*, without abandoning blindness [*avijjā*], without giving rise to knowledge, he will make an end of *dukkha* in this very life—there is no such possibility.
mn148:29-33.2: dependent on the nose, *bhikkhus*, and odors, nose-*viññāṇa* arises …
mn148:29-33.3: dependent on the tongue, *bhikkhus*, and tastes, tongue-*viññāṇa* arises …
mn148:29-33.4: dependent on the body, *bhikkhus*, and tangibles, body-*viññāṇa* arises …
mn148:29-33.5: dependent on the mind, *bhikkhus*, and *dhammas*, mind-*viññāṇa* arises; the meeting of the three is contact; with contact as condition there arises what is felt: *sukha* or *dukkha* or neither-*dukkha*-nor-*sukha*.
mn148:29-33.6: Touched by *sukha vedanā*, he delights in it, welcomes it, and remains holding to it.
mn148:29-33.7: His underlying tendency to passion underlies it.
mn148:29-33.8: Touched by *dukkha vedanā*, he sorrows, becomes weary, laments, beats his breast, cries, and falls into confusion.
mn148:29-33.9: His underlying tendency to resistance underlies it.
mn148:3.1: “Six internal dimensions [*āyatanāni*] should be understood; six external dimensions [*āyatanāni*] should be understood; six bodies of *viññāṇa* should be understood; six bodies of contact should be understood; six bodies of *vedanā* should be understood; six bodies of *taṇhā* should be understood.
mn148:34.1: Dependent on the eye, *bhikkhus*, and forms [*rūpe*], eye-*viññāṇa* arises; the meeting of the three is contact; with contact as condition there arises what is felt: *sukha* or *dukkha* or neither-*dukkha*-nor-*sukha*.
mn148:34.2: Touched by *sukha vedanā*, he does not delight in it, does not welcome it, and does not remain holding to it.
mn148:34.3: His underlying tendency to passion does not underlie it.
mn148:34.4: Touched by *dukkha vedanā*, he does not sorrow, does not become weary, does not lament, does not beat his breast and cry, and does not fall into confusion.
mn148:34.5: His underlying tendency to resistance does not underlie it.
mn148:34.6: Touched by neither-*dukkha*-nor-*sukha vedanā*, he understands as it truly is the arising, passing away, gratification, danger, and escape in regard to that *vedanā*.
mn148:34.7: His underlying tendency to blindness [*avijjā*] does not underlie it.
mn148:34.8: That, *bhikkhus*, having abandoned the underlying tendency to passion toward *sukha vedanā*, having driven out the underlying tendency to resistance toward *dukkha vedanā*, having uprooted the underlying tendency to blindness [*avijjā*] toward neither-*dukkha*-nor-*sukha vedanā*, having abandoned blindness [*avijjā*], having given rise to knowledge, he will make an end of *dukkha* in this very life—this possibility exists.
mn148:35.1: Dependent on the ear, *bhikkhus*, and sounds, ear-*viññāṇa* arises …
mn148:36.1: Dependent on the nose, *bhikkhus*, and odors, nose-*viññāṇa* arises …
mn148:37.1: Dependent on the tongue, *bhikkhus*, and tastes, tongue-*viññāṇa* arises …
mn148:38.1: Dependent on the body, *bhikkhus*, and tangibles, body-*viññāṇa* arises …
mn148:39.1: “Dependent on the mind, *bhikkhus*, and *dhammas*, mind-*viññāṇa* arises; the meeting of the three is contact; with contact as condition there arises what is felt: *sukha* or *dukkha* or neither-*dukkha*-nor-*sukha*.
mn148:39.2: Touched by *sukha vedanā*, he does not delight in it, does not welcome it, and does not remain holding to it.
mn148:39.3: His underlying tendency to passion does not underlie it.
mn148:39.4: Touched by *dukkha vedanā*, he does not sorrow, does not become weary, does not lament, does not beat his breast and cry, and does not fall into confusion.
mn148:39.5: His underlying tendency to resistance does not underlie it.
mn148:39.6: Touched by neither-*dukkha*-nor-*sukha vedanā*, he understands as it truly is the arising, passing away, gratification, danger, and escape in regard to that *vedanā*.
mn148:39.7: His underlying tendency to blindness [*avijjā*] does not underlie it.
mn148:39.8: That, *bhikkhus*, having abandoned the underlying tendency to passion toward *sukha vedanā*, having driven out the underlying tendency to resistance toward *dukkha vedanā*, having uprooted the underlying tendency to blindness [*avijjā*] toward neither-*dukkha*-nor-*sukha vedanā*, having abandoned blindness [*avijjā*], having given rise to knowledge, he will make an end of *dukkha* in this very life—this possibility exists.
mn148:4.2: so it was said. And in reference to what was this said?
mn148:4.3: The eye dimension [*cakkhāyatanaṁ*], the ear dimension [*sotāyatanaṁ*], the nose dimension [*ghānāyatanaṁ*], the tongue dimension [*jivhāyatanaṁ*], the body dimension [*kāyāyatanaṁ*], the mind dimension [*manāyatanaṁ*].
mn148:4.5: that which was said was said in reference to this.
mn148:40.1: Seeing thus, *bhikkhus*, a learned noble disciple becomes disenchanted with the eye, becomes disenchanted with forms [*rūpesu*], becomes disenchanted with eye-*viññāṇa*, becomes disenchanted with eye-contact, becomes disenchanted with *vedanā*, becomes disenchanted with *taṇhā*.
mn148:41.1: He becomes disenchanted with the ear, becomes disenchanted with sounds …
mn148:41.10: And while this exposition was being spoken, the minds [*cittāni*] of about sixty *bhikkhus* were liberated from the *āsavas* without *upādāna* [*anupādāya*].
mn148:41.11: The Six Sixes is finished, the sixth.
mn148:41.2: he becomes disenchanted with the nose, becomes disenchanted with odors …
mn148:41.3: he becomes disenchanted with the tongue, becomes disenchanted with tastes …
mn148:41.4: he becomes disenchanted with the body, becomes disenchanted with tangibles …
mn148:41.5: he becomes disenchanted with the mind, becomes disenchanted with *dhammas*, becomes disenchanted with mind-*viññāṇa*, becomes disenchanted with mind-contact, becomes disenchanted with *vedanā*, becomes disenchanted with *taṇhā*.
mn148:41.6: Being disenchanted, he becomes dispassionate; through dispassion he is liberated. When liberated, there is the knowledge, ‘It is liberated.’
mn148:41.7: He understands: ‘Birth is destroyed, the holy life has been lived, what had to be done has been done, there is no more of this state of being.’”
mn148:41.9: Satisfied, those *bhikkhus* delighted in the *Bhagavā*'s statement.
mn148:5.2: so it was said. And in reference to what was this said?
mn148:5.3: The dimension of forms [*rūpāyatanaṁ*], the dimension of sounds [*saddāyatanaṁ*], the dimension of odors [*gandhāyatanaṁ*], the dimension of tastes [*rasāyatanaṁ*], the dimension of tangibles [*phoṭṭhabbāyatanaṁ*], the dimension of *dhammas* [*dhammāyatanaṁ*].
mn148:5.5: that which was said was said in reference to this.

mn148:6.8: dependent on the mind and *dhammas*, mind-*viññāṇa* arises.
